sets.q: A pipe delivers 0.6 m³ of water in 3 seconds. What is the volumetric flow rate?
A faucet releases 1.5 m³ of water in 5 seconds. What is the flow rate?
A hose discharges 2.4 m³ in 6 seconds. What is the volumetric flow rate?
A pump moves 0.9 m³ of fluid in 3 seconds. Find the flow rate.
A tank releases 5 m³ of water in 10 seconds. What is the volumetric flow rate?
Water flows through a pipe of cross-sectional area 0.005 m² at a velocity of 4 m/s. What is the flow rate?
Fluid flows at 3 m/s through a pipe with cross-section 0.008 m². What is the flow rate?
A pipe with cross-section 0.01 m² carries water at 5 m/s. What is the volumetric flow rate?
Water flows at 6 m/s through a pipe of area 0.002 m². Find Q.
A nozzle with area 0.003 m² ejects water at 10 m/s. What is the flow rate?
The Continuity Equation states that for an incompressible fluid in a pipe:
sets.given: This is the principle of conservation of mass in fluid flow.
opts: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂ (flow rate is constant throughout)
A₁v₁ > A₂v₂ (flow rate increases in wider sections)
Velocity is the same everywhere in the pipe
Pressure is the same everywhere in the pipe

sets.q: What does the Continuity Equation (A₁v₁ = A₂v₂) express?
sets.given: Think about what is conserved in fluid flow.
opts: Conservation of mass (volume flow rate is constant)

sets.q: In which type of pipe does fluid flow faster according to the Continuity Equation?
sets.given: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂. If A₂ < A₁, then v₂ = ?

sets.q: Water enters a wide pipe section and flows into a narrow section. What happens to the speed?
sets.given: Continuity: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂. Smaller A means ?
opts: Speed increases in the narrow section
Speed decreases in the narrow section
Speed becomes zero in the narrow section

sets.q: The Continuity Equation assumes the fluid is:
sets.given: The equation A₁v₁ = A₂v₂ is derived assuming no gaps or compressions.

sets.q: Water flows at 2 m/s through a pipe of area 0.1 m². It narrows to 0.02 m². What is the velocity in the narrow section?
sets.given:
Continuity: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂
Solve: v₂ = A₁v₁ / A₂

sets.q: Water flows at 3 m/s in a pipe of area 0.06 m². It narrows to 0.03 m². Find the velocity in the narrow section.
A pipe narrows from area 0.08 m² (where v = 1.5 m/s) to 0.04 m². What is the velocity in the narrow section?
Water moves at 4 m/s through a pipe of area 0.05 m². The pipe widens to 0.1 m². Find the new velocity.
A pipe with area 0.03 m² carries water at 5 m/s. It expands to 0.15 m². What is the velocity in the wider section?
How much volume of water flows per second through a pipe of area 0.04 m² at a velocity of 3 m/s?
A fire hose with cross-section 0.007 m² sprays water at 8 m/s. What is the flow rate?
A river cross-section is 5 m² and the current flows at 2 m/s. What is the volumetric flow rate?
A water pipe (area 0.015 m²) delivers water at 4 m/s. What is the flow rate in m³/s?
An irrigation channel is 0.5 m wide and 0.4 m deep, flowing at 1.5 m/s. What is the volumetric flow rate?
sets.given: A = width × depth; Q = A × v

sets.q: A flow rate of 0.05 m³/s enters a pipe of area 0.01 m². What is the fluid velocity?
Q = 0.09 m³/s and A = 0.03 m². Find the velocity.
A pump delivers Q = 0.04 m³/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 0.008 m². What is the flow velocity?
Water flows at Q = 0.12 m³/s through a pipe of area 0.04 m². What is the velocity?
A flow rate of 0.025 m³/s enters a 0.005 m² nozzle. What is the exit velocity?
What area pipe is needed to carry Q = 0.06 m³/s at a velocity of 3 m/s?
sets.given: Rearrange Q = A × v: A = Q / v

sets.q: Q = 0.10 m³/s, v = 5 m/s. What cross-sectional area is required?
A system requires Q = 0.12 m³/s at v = 4 m/s. Find the pipe cross-section.
Q = 0.08 m³/s, v = 8 m/s. What is the required pipe area?
To deliver Q = 0.15 m³/s at v = 3 m/s, what pipe area is required?
What volume of water flows through a pipe of area 0.02 m² at 6 m/s in 10 seconds?
sets.given:
Q = A × v
V = Q × t

sets.q: A pipe of area 0.005 m² carries water at 4 m/s. How much water flows in 30 seconds?
sets.given: Q = A × v; V = Q × t

sets.q: A nozzle with area 0.001 m² sprays water at 10 m/s. How much water is delivered in 60 seconds?
sets.given: Q = A × v; V = Q × t

sets.q: A river (cross-section 10 m²) flows at 0.5 m/s. How much water passes a point in 1 minute?
sets.given: Q = A × v; V = Q × t (t = 60 s)

sets.q: A pipe (area 0.025 m²) carries water at 8 m/s. How much water is delivered in 5 seconds?
sets.given: Q = A × v; V = Q × t

sets.q: Pipe A has area 0.1 m² and velocity 2 m/s. It connects to Pipe B with area 0.05 m². What is the velocity in Pipe B?
A₁ = 0.08 m², v₁ = 3 m/s. Pipe narrows to A₂ = 0.04 m². Find v₂.
A₁ = 0.04 m², v₁ = 5 m/s. Pipe widens to A₂ = 0.20 m². Find v₂.
A river narrows from 10 m² to 2 m². If the wide section flows at 0.5 m/s, what is the speed in the narrow section?
Water enters a junction pipe at A₁ = 0.06 m², v₁ = 4 m/s. The outlet area is A₂ = 0.12 m². Find the outlet velocity.
How long does it take to fill a 3 m³ tank at a flow rate of 0.5 m³/s?
sets.given: Rearrange Q = V / t: t = V / Q

sets.q: A tank of volume 12 m³ is filled at Q = 0.4 m³/s. How long does it take?
A swimming pool holds 200 m³. A pipe delivers Q = 0.02 m³/s. How long to fill it?
A 0.6 m³ aquarium is filled at Q = 0.003 m³/s. How long does it take?
A 5 m³ water tank drains at Q = 0.25 m³/s. How long does it take to empty?
